metadata.description: Not used, but needed to pass arm-ttk test `Location-Should-Not-Be-Hardcoded`.  We instead use the `workspace-location` which is derived from the LA workspace
[concat('Region to deploy solution resources -- separate from location selection',parameters('location'))]
Workspace name for Log Analytics where Microsoft Sentinel is setup

properties.description: FunctionApp data connector with template version 3.0.0

connectorUiConfig.title: Function App Sample (Ingestion API)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The **Function App Sample** data connector demonstrates how to ingest custom JSON events into a Microsoft Sentinel custom table using an Azure Function and the [Azure Monitor Ingestion API](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/logs-ingestion-api-overview) (DCE/DCR). Use this as a starting template for building your own Azure Function–based connectors.

sampleQueries.query:
FunctionAppSample_CL
| where EventType == "Alert"
| sort by TimeGenerated desc

dataTypes.name: FunctionAppSample_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
FunctionAppSample_CL
            | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
            | where isnotempty(Time)

value:
FunctionAppSample_CL
            | summarize LastLogReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
            | project IsConnected = LastLogReceived > ago(1d)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and write permissions on the workspace are required.

customs.name: Microsoft.Web/sites permissions
customs.description: Read and write permissions to Azure Functions to create a Function App is required. [See the documentation to learn more about Azure Functions](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-functions/).

customs.name: Azure AD App Registration
customs.description: An Azure AD App Registration (service principal) is required. After the connector is deployed you will manually assign the **Monitoring Metrics Publisher** role on the Data Collection Rule (DCR) to this App Registration (see Step 1). [Learn more about registering an application](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/quickstart-register-app).

instructionSteps.description: >**NOTE:** This connector uses Azure Functions and the Azure Monitor Ingestion API (DCE/DCR) to push sample events into Microsoft Sentinel. The ARM template creates the Data Collection Endpoint, custom log table (`FunctionAppSample_CL`), Data Collection Rule, and Function App. After deployment, a **Monitoring Metrics Publisher** role assignment on the DCR must be completed manually — see Step 1. Supplied credentials are stored in **Azure Key Vault** by the deployment; the Function App reads them via Key Vault references — no plain-text secrets in application settings. This might result in additional data ingestion costs. Check the [Azure Functions pricing page](https://azure.microsoft.com/pricing/details/functions/) and [Azure Monitor pricing page](https://azure.microsoft.com/pricing/details/monitor/) for details.

>**(Optional Step)** Securely store your App Registration credentials in Azure Key Vault. [Follow these instructions](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/app-service/app-service-key-vault-references) to use Azure Key Vault references with an Azure Function App.

1. In the [Azure Portal](https://portal.azure.com), navigate to **Azure Active Directory > App registrations > New registration**.
2. Provide a name (e.g. `FunctionApp-Sentinel-Connector`) and register.
3. Under **Certificates & secrets**, create a new client secret. Note the **Tenant ID**, **Client ID**, and **Client Secret** — these are required in Step 2.

**After completing Step 2**, return here to grant the App Registration access to push data into Sentinel:

4. Open the deployed **Data Collection Rule** in the Azure Portal (search by name in the resource group used in Step 2, or find it in the deployment outputs).
5. Go to **Access control (IAM) > Add role assignment**.
6. Select the **Monitoring Metrics Publisher** role.
7. Under **Members**, choose **User, group, or service principal** and select the App Registration created above.
8. Click **Review + assign** and wait a few minutes for RBAC propagation before verifying ingestion.
instructionSteps.title: STEP 1 – Create an Azure AD App Registration

instructionSteps.description:
Click **Deploy to Azure** and fill in the parameters. The template will create the Data Collection Endpoint, `FunctionAppSample_CL` table, Data Collection Rule, and Function App.

[![Deploy To Azure](https://aka.ms/deploytoazurebutton)](https://portal.azure.com/#create/Microsoft.Template/uri/https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2Frobertmoriarty12%2FSentinel-Ingestion-Accelerator-Function-App%2Fmain%2FData%2520Connectors%2Fazuredeploy_FunctionApp_API_FunctionApp.json)

**Parameters to fill in:**

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| `WorkspaceName` | Name of your Log Analytics / Sentinel workspace |
| `TenantId` | Azure AD Tenant ID from Step 1 |
| `ClientId` | App Registration Client ID from Step 1 |
| `ClientSecret` | App Registration Client Secret from Step 1 — stored in Azure Key Vault by the deployment |
| `AppInsightsWorkspaceResourceID` | Full Resource ID of the Log Analytics workspace (from **Log Analytics workspace > Properties > Resource ID**) |
| `FunctionAppLocation` | Azure region for the Function App and related resources. Can differ from the Resource Group location (e.g. set `centralus` even if your RG is in `westus`). Defaults to the Resource Group location if left blank. |
instructionSteps.title: STEP 2 – Deploy the Azure Function App

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

The Function App Sample solution demonstrates how to ingest custom JSON events into a Microsoft Sentinel custom table using an Azure Function and the Azure Monitor Ingestion API (DCE/DCR). Use this as a starting template for building your own Azure Function–based data connectors.

Underlying Microsoft Technologies used:

This solution takes a dependency on the following technologies, and some of these dependencies either may be in Preview state or might result in additional ingestion or operational costs:

  1. Azure Monitor Ingestion API (DCE/DCR)

  2. Azure Functions

Data Connectors: 1

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
